# Function to check and install missing packages
install_if_missing <- function(pkg) {
  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages(pkg, dependencies=TRUE, repos="http://cran.rstudio.com/")
  }
  library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
}

# Install and load necessary packages
install_if_missing("rpart")
install_if_missing("rpart.plot")
install_if_missing("pROC")
install_if_missing("caret")
install_if_missing("dplyr")
install_if_missing("e1071")  # Required dependency for caret
install_if_missing("fastDummies")  # Required for one-hot encoding
install_if_missing("ggplot2")  # For visualization
install_if_missing("gridExtra")  # For multiple plots
install_if_missing("MLmetrics")
library(MLmetrics)  # For additional evaluation metrics
install_if_missing("glmnet")  # For LASSO regression

# Special handling for randomForest
if (!requireNamespace("randomForest", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("randomForest", dependencies=TRUE, type="binary", repos="http://cran.rstudio.com/")
}
library(randomForest)

# Load dataset
data <- read.csv("small_lending_club_clean.csv")
data$loan_status <- as.factor(data$loan_status)

# Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)
ggplot(data, aes(x=loan_status)) + geom_bar(fill="blue") + theme_minimal() + ggtitle("Distribution of Loan Status")
ggplot(data, aes(x=int_rate)) + geom_histogram(fill="red", bins=30) + theme_minimal() + ggtitle("Interest Rate Distribution")

# Data Preprocessing
columns_to_drop <- c("id", "pymnt_plan", "url", "zip_code", "initial_list_status", "policy_code", "application_type", "acc_now_delinq", "delinq_amnt")
data <- data %>% select(-all_of(columns_to_drop))
data <- na.omit(data)  # Remove missing values

# Split data into training, validation, and holdout sets
set.seed(123)
num_rows <- nrow(data)
rows_to_move <- sample(1:num_rows, size = ceiling(0.2 * num_rows))

holdout_set <- data[rows_to_move, ]   # 20% for final evaluation
work_set <- data[-rows_to_move, ]      # Remaining 80% for training & validation

# Implement 4-Fold Cross-Validation
k <- 4
row_indices <- sample(1:nrow(work_set))
folds <- cut(1:nrow(work_set), breaks = k, labels = FALSE)

fold_accuracy <- numeric(k)

for(i in 1:k) {
  test_indices <- row_indices[folds == i]
  train_data <- work_set[-test_indices, ]
  test_data <- work_set[test_indices, ]

  # Convert categorical variables properly
  for (col in names(train_data)) {
    if (is.character(train_data[[col]]) || is.logical(train_data[[col]])) {
      train_data[[col]] <- as.factor(train_data[[col]])
      test_data[[col]] <- factor(test_data[[col]], levels = levels(train_data[[col]]))
    }
  }
  
  # Ensure numeric scaling
  numeric_cols <- names(train_data)[sapply(train_data, is.numeric)]
  numeric_cols <- numeric_cols[sapply(train_data[numeric_cols], function(x) var(x, na.rm=TRUE) > 0)]
  preprocess_params <- preProcess(train_data[numeric_cols], method = c("center", "scale"))
  train_data[numeric_cols] <- predict(preprocess_params, train_data[numeric_cols])
  test_data[numeric_cols] <- predict(preprocess_params, test_data[numeric_cols])

  # Train models
  cart_model <- rpart(loan_status ~ ., data = train_data, maxdepth = 4)
  log_model <- train(loan_status ~ ., data = train_data, method = "glm", family = binomial)
  rf_model <- randomForest(loan_status ~ ., data = train_data, ntree = 100)

  # Make predictions
  cart_pred <- predict(cart_model, newdata = test_data, type = "class")
  log_pred <- predict(log_model, newdata = test_data)
  rf_pred <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data)

  # Convert Logistic Regression Predictions
  log_pred <- as.factor(ifelse(log_pred > 0.5, "Charged Off", "Fully Paid"))
  
  # Compute Accuracy
  cart_acc <- confusionMatrix(cart_pred, test_data$loan_status)$overall["Accuracy"]
  log_acc <- confusionMatrix(log_pred, test_data$loan_status)$overall["Accuracy"]
  rf_acc <- confusionMatrix(rf_pred, test_data$loan_status)$overall["Accuracy"]

  cat("Fold", i, "- CART Accuracy:", cart_acc, "| Logistic Accuracy:", log_acc, "| Random Forest Accuracy:", rf_acc, "\n")
  flush.console()
  fold_accuracy[i] <- mean(c(cart_acc, log_acc, rf_acc))
}

# Compute average Accuracy
mean_accuracy <- mean(fold_accuracy)
cat("Average 4-Fold Accuracy:", mean_accuracy, "\n")

# Final Model Evaluation on Holdout Set
best_model <- rpart(loan_status ~ ., data = work_set, maxdepth = 4)
predicted_holdout <- predict(best_model, newdata = holdout_set, type = "class")
holdout_cm <- confusionMatrix(predicted_holdout, holdout_set$loan_status)
holdout_accuracy <- holdout_cm$overall["Accuracy"]

cat("Holdout Set Accuracy:", holdout_accuracy, "\n")
print(holdout_cm)
